queries.query: ok our cli's --help output is dense af, our team keeps complaining new joiners can't figure out what --region vs --zone vs --cluster actually does. can you take a pass at the help layout and tell me what to tighten? the cli is called 'flask-deploy' if that matters

we're shipping a 1.0 of a public SDK for our payments API in python and node next quarter. what are the top api contracts / surface decisions i should pin down before 1.0 so we don't end up with a hyrum's-law nightmare a year in?

users keep reporting they retry our webhooks because our endpoint sometimes returns HTTP 200 with a body like {error: 'invalid signature'}. our PM says 'isn't that fine, the body says error?' how do i explain why it's not?

before we cut 2.0 of our grpc billing service, can you sanity-check that we have a deprecation window for the v1 paths and that our migration docs mention what an integrator has to do? we have like 30 enterprise customers on v1 still
i have this onboarding doc for new contributors to our open-source repo (about 8 steps from clone to first PR). can you tell me where new people will get stuck? we keep losing first-time contributors
customer keeps hitting `auth failed` after they rotate api keys in our dashboard. they swear they're using the new key. what should we look at?

i want to redesign the error envelope for our REST API. right now half the endpoints return {error: 'string'} and the other half return {code, message, details}. what shape should it take if i want clients to handle retries and idempotency cleanly?
review this terraform module's variable docs and outputs — i want to know if a developer who's never seen this module before could use it without reading the source
our developers complain that pnpm install takes 45s, the rollup build is 2m, and the jest tests are 8m. before we throw budget at it, what's worth investing in first from a dx perspective?
design the plugin api for our cli so third parties can register subcommands without forking the binary. similar shape to how kubectl plugins work or how npx scripts get wired
verify whether DX-CLI-002 is fixed using the saved workflow-state json and this PR diff
our README is mostly marketing copy and badges and people keep asking us in support what the install command is. can you audit it for first-impressions stuff?
do a 30-second basics pass on this repo — does it have a working README install snippet, a LICENSE, a CHANGELOG, --help and --version actually working, etc. quick gut check before we ship

we keep getting customers blowing up because `npm install our-sdk` pulls in like 80MB of transitive deps and our peer deps aren't declared so it silently breaks on react 17. can you audit the package surface?
we don't have a samples directory at all and our docs only have inline code snippets. before launch, what should a proper examples surface look like for an SDK shipping in 3 languages?
users say our cli's --verbose flag dumps json on top of regular output and they can't grep it, plus they think it's leaking their api key. can you take a look at our logging UX?
i need to design how our cli reads config — file vs env vs flag precedence, where the config file lives on linux/mac/windows, and how we validate. what should we get right up front?
our CHANGELOG.md is basically auto-generated commit messages and breaking changes are just buried in there with no callout. can you tell us how to fix the changelog surface?

claude code keeps tripping on our repo — it doesn't know our test commands, it tries `git push --force` and gets blocked, it generates AGENTS.md autogenerated content that's wrong. how do we make this repo agent-friendly?

we're building a TS client SDK that wraps an LLM API — streaming completions, tool calls, structured output via zod. before alpha, what's the dx checklist? specifically worried about streaming altitudes and how we handle validation failures on structured outputs

designing an agent SDK in python — the loop is request -> tool -> request and i want to give callers stopWhen-style predicates plus pre-tool hooks for human approval. what's the shape that won't paint us into a corner?

user reports our AI sdk's structured output 'works most of the time' but sometimes throws on JSON parse with no retry, and the agent loop occasionally runs 40 turns and burns $12 of tokens before stopping. where would you look?
users on alpine linux keep saying our pip install fails because libxml2 isn't a declared system requirement and the error message just says 'gcc not found'. is this a package or a setup problem?
explain what JWT is and how RS256 differs from HS256, i'm writing a one-pager for our security team
rewrite this paragraph in our help center to be friendlier and less corporate: 'Our refund policy requires customers to submit requests within 14 calendar days of the original transaction.'
refactor this typescript function to use early returns instead of nested if statements — function exportInvoices(items, opts) { /* 30 lines */ }
what's the best way to compare graphql versus rest in a tech-decision document for our cto? i need pros and cons for each
design a new homepage hero section for our marketing site, should be a clear value prop. we sell developer tools but the hero is for general buyers, not devs
review my python script `import_orders.py` for style and pep8 issues
our user-facing signup flow at example.com/signup has a 60% drop-off rate, help me reduce friction. it's a 4-step form
what's the difference between an internal vs external rest api in our microservices arch? trying to understand the boundaries
audit the accessibility of our checkout page for WCAG 2.1 AA compliance, here's the page html
the production database is hot at 2pm every day on our reporting query, help me trace what's causing it. it's postgres 14 with pgbouncer
